title: Fluffy Korean Mozzarella Songpyeon
description: A tangy and fluffy post-workout Korean dish featuring mozzarella balls, flambe, and vegetarian-friendly ingredients in a traditional songpyeon form, cooked in a cast-iron skillet.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the shredded mozzarella cheese, all-purpose flour, cornstarch, and salt, and mix well.
Gradually add the sesame oil, soy sauce, and rice vinegar to the bowl, and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for about 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces, and shape each piece into a small ball.
Heat a cast-iron skillet over medium heat, and add a small amount of sesame oil to the skillet.
Add the mozzarella balls to the skillet, and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the mozzarella balls from the skillet, and set them aside on a plate.
In the same skillet, add a small amount of soy sauce and rice vinegar, and bring to a boil.
Add the chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds to the skillet, and stir-fry for about 1 minute.
Return the mozzarella balls to the skillet, and toss them in the soy sauce and green onion mixture.
Flambe the mozzarella balls by adding a small amount of sesame oil to the skillet, and igniting it with a match or lighter.
Serve the fluffy Korean mozzarella songpyeon hot, garnished with additional green onions and toasted sesame seeds if desired.
